desires: Maintain the seamless, friction-free connection with cohort_3 without triggering any tension or feeling of obligation to prioritize my term project over their immediate reassurance.
Avoid appearing as the person who breaks the group's current dynamic by suddenly demanding attention for a task that feels distant and unconnected to our shared comfort right now.
Preserve the long-term payoff of this deepening bond, even if it means allowing the deadline to feel slightly less urgent than usual, rather than risking a momentary spike in conflict.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for our scheduled meal tonight to reinforce group cohesion and show I'm still committed to the project.
goals.rationale: My immediate anxiety stems from feeling like I'm abandoning the group; by prioritizing this meal, I secure their trust and avoid the friction of appearing unreliable. Maintaining this emotional buffer is more urgent than any abstract deadline.

goals.description: Complete the trivial chore just enough to satisfy the immediate notification without expending significant mental energy.
goals.rationale: I want this small win, but since protecting my friends' trust is currently more urgent than my own low-stakes victory, I'm pushing this back until after the meal. Doing it now might feel like an intrusion on my needed downtime, whereas doing it later ensures I don't feel guilty about prioritizing others over myself in the moment.

goals.rationale: I need time to reflect on how to handle the deadline discussion without creating immediate friction. Pushing this further away keeps the conversation low-pressure for me while I wait for the inevitable moment to speak up, ensuring I can present myself as thoughtful rather than evasive.
